title: STRLEN
description: Returns the length of a string value.

returns.description: Long integer-reply the length of the string at key, or 0 when key does not exist.
Long integer-reply the length of the string at key, or 0 when key does not exist.

returns.description: The length of the string at key, or 0 when key does not exist.
the length of the value stored in the key, or 0 if the key does not exist

content:
Returns the length of the string value stored at `key`. An error is returned when `key` holds a non-string value. ## Examples {{% redis-cli %}} SET mykey "Hello world" STRLEN mykey STRLEN nonexisting {{% /redis-cli %}} ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="strlen-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the length of the string stored at key, or 0 when the key does not exist. -tab-sep- [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the length of the string stored at key, or 0 when the key does not exist. {{< /multitabs >}}
example:
SET mykey "Hello world"
STRLEN mykey
STRLEN nonexisting
intro:
Returns the length of the string value stored at `key`.
An error is returned when `key` holds a non-string value.
